transcript.clean_text: and so at last the queen stage of this year's welter the run to gar monetero well if this was a daunting task it was a light mood considering what we had already completed in this welter yesterday's run to covadonga was tough this would be worse we're climbing almost immediately out of the back door two category one tests and a category two to introduce us to the defining stage of the race the mighty bounce with ramps of 17 and more at the end of a day was surely going to draw out a breakaway and in the end it was a huge one 32 riders went up the road and we were assuming that this would be two battles on the day one amongst the gc men and one from the break while missing out on the break were bahrain victorious and they set the pace a tough one called for by jack hague he wanted to shake out as many of his rivals as possible and hopefully move up onto a podium position well the breakaway itself did begin to fracture and this man michael storer not content with two stage wins to his name he was now hunting down king of the mountains points well he got so many of them he actually overtook his teammate roman bardet in that competition meanwhile as the kilometers were ticking by stora was winning mountains points but there was a stage to be won and still no spanish winner on this year's welter would that change david de la cruz had ideas and ua team emirates were starting to help charge down those up the road store still out ahead who would stop him not by a day that's for sure who seated that jersey to his teammate plenty more battles and mountains points still to come in that competition well we had some brave brave moves and david de la cruz was one of those he set off to try and wind in store he found him but so did the favorites find their pace and in fact when we came to the final ramps of the mighty climb that defined the day the pace was down but the gaps were narrowing de la cruz asked for a turn from store who said absolutely not i've been out here on my own for much of the day and it was a very selective group of chasers that set off for glory but now yet again the usual suspects from the day prior he and roglic locked together yates and monkeys also in the frame but one man stretched out for glory miguel anger lopez wanted to cheer up spain he is of course colombian but movie star wanting a win and he was to deliver it first he wound in de la cruz a narrow margin on the red jersey of just about half a minute would it be enough or with two kilometers to go we knew that lopez was going to take the day but how would it pan out behind roglich was in determined mood so was bernal and likewise mass but now yet again went on the charge mouse not giving either of them a turn roglic biding his time the day's victory was going to miguel anger lopez out of the fog he emerged and pushing into the clouds who would be in second place the answer was the leader of this race primos rockledge finding a gap and indeed bonus seconds on his nearest rival mouse as he crossed the line mask coming home and knowing that a podium place surely is all he's going to take from this race brave it was by gila mana and jack hague they coming in with de la cruz and louis mackeys who produced a spectacular performance on the day so the big climbing tests back to back have now been complete rolling stages and some climbing remain before the time trial roglic looks in charge not today though megalanga lopez taking a much needed victory for movie star roger's in second place ahead of mass bernal hague rounding out the top five de la cruz murder menkes and cuss also there we have our leader we have our champion in waiting the time trial will favor him that's for sure and he has an enhanced gap overall of two and a half minutes over mass the gap between mass and his teammate lopez narrowing to 23 seconds with hagen fourth ahead of egan bernard a new leader of the king of the mountains competition and with plenty of climbing points still up for grabs not least straight out of the back door tomorrow this could be a tight competition but it looks like dsm are going to defend it either storer or bar day should be a king of the mountain by the time this race closes on so to stage 19 montford lemos well they're climbing straight out of the back door it's a day that surely a breaker may must own and look at that the calorie three and then two category twos with rolling terrain leading to a fast finish off the sprint it will be dramatic every day is in this race who will win well you'll have to tune in to find out you
